description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on the ubuntu:bionic Docker image and sets up an APT cache directory in before_script, then updates apt and installs autoconf, automake, autotools-dev, gobject-introspection, gtk-doc-tools, libdbus-1-dev, libgirepository1.0-dev, libglib2.0-dev, pkg-config, valac, python3, python3-gi, python-gi-dev, lcov, gcovr, check, libsqlite3-dev, git and meson using the cache. Define three stages: build, test, and deploy. In the build stage add a build_amd64 job that runs `export PYTHON=python3`, `NOCONFIGURE=1 ./autogen.sh`, `mkdir build`, `cd build`, `../configure --enable-gtk-doc --enable-introspection=yes --enable-tests --enable-python --enable-coverage`, and `make -j 4`, and archives the whole repository as an artifact. In the test stage add a test_amd64 job that depends on build_amd64 and runs `git clone https://gitlab.com/accounts-sso/gsignond.git`, `cd gsignond`, `meson build --prefix=/usr -Ddebug=true`, `ninja -C build install`, `cd ..`, `find build | xargs touch`, `cd build`, `make check || (cat tests/test-suite.log && /bin/false)`, and `make lcov`, also storing the repository as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a pages job that depends on test_amd64, runs only on tags, executes `mkdir public`, `cp -a build/lcov-report public/coverage`, `cp -a build/docs/reference/html/* public`, and publishes the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact. Use a cache with key apt-cache that saves the apt-cache/ directory.
